metadata.author: Hosea
metadata.placeOfWriting: In the Northern Kingdom of Israel
metadata.subject: Jehovah as the Husband of His People, Who are Rebellious and Adulterous, yet He is Long-suffering and Redeeming.

1: The word of Jehovah that came to Hosea the son of Beeri, in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam the son of Joash, king of Israel.

When Jehovah first spoke through Hosea, Jehovah said to Hosea, Go, take to yourself a wife of harlotries and children of harlotries, for the land commits great harlotry by departing from following Jehovah.
2: Therefore, behold, I will allure her and bring her into the wilderness and speak to her heart.
And I will betroth you to Me forever; yes, I will betroth you to Me in righteousness and in justice and in lovingkindness and in compassions.
6: Come and let us return to Jehovah; for He has torn us, but He will heal us; He has stricken us, but He will bind us up.
For I delight in lovingkindness and not sacrifice, and the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings.
11: When Israel was a child, I loved him, and out of Egypt I called My son.
I drew them with cords of a man, with bands of love; and I was to them like those who lift the yoke from their jaws; and I bent down to them and fed them.
14: I will heal their backsliding; I will love them freely, for My anger has turned away from him.
I will be like the dew to Israel; he will blossom like the lily and strike his roots like the cedars of Lebanon.

19.text: The relationship between Jehovah and His people is depicted as a marriage. Despite Israel's unfaithfulness, God's ultimate intention is to restore this union in righteousness and love.

4.text: This signifies God's dealing with His people through human channels and affections, especially seen in the incarnation of Christ where God reached man in a human way.

5.text: The dew represents the refreshing and life-giving grace of God that comes in the morning of restoration after the dark night of judgment.
